metadata.title: How Do Nonlinear Transformers Acquire Generalization-Guaranteed CoT Ability?

sections.title: Overview
content: Theoretically characterize how training Transformers can enable chain-of-thought (CoT).
Quantitative analysis of how context examples affect CoT performance.
Theoretical study of why CoT outperforms ICL.

sections.title: Background and motivation
content: CoT augments K-step reasoning examples to the query for generation, i.e., an extension of in-context learning (ICL) by multi-step examples.
Existing works [1,2,3]: the expressive power.
The problem ofwhy a Transformer can be trained to conduct CoTis less investigated.

sections.title: Problem formulation
content: Study learning K-step reasoning tasks. Each task f is a composition of {fᵢ} and outputs {zᵢ} for the input z₀.
Learning model: one-layer Transformer F(P) = Σ Wᵥ p̃ᵢ softmax( (Wₖ p̃ᵢ)ᵀ W_Q p̃_query ), where P is the input prompt.
Training:Following theoretical works [4,5] for ICL, we aim to solve an empirical risk minimization by stochastic gradient descent with squared loss.
CoT Inference:The testing prompt consists of examples and a query. CoT repeats two steps for K steps:
Greedy decoding to generate the next step.
Add the generation to the end of the prompt.
ICL Inference:The testing examples only contain the input and the final-step output.
[1] Li et al. Disscting Chain-of-Thought. NeurIPS 2023. [2] Feng et al. Towards Revealing the Mystery. NeurIPS 2023. [3] Li et al. Chain of Thought Empowers Transformers. ICLR 2024.

sections.title: Theoretical results
content: Data & Tasks: The training and testing data are formulated by TTR/TSR patterns. Considererroneous testing prompts: reasoning in some examples contains incorrect steps.
Theorem 1 (Convergence):With the number of context examples and iterations linear in Ω(α⁻¹), the population risk is ≤ O(ε).
Theorem 2 (CoT Generalization):To achieve a zero CoT error, the number of context examples for task f is proportional to (α' τᶠ ρᶠ)⁻².
Theorem 3 (Comparison with ICL):The success of ICL requires a dominant fraction of correct input-label examples (Condition 1), but CoT does not.
The mechanism of CoTProposition 1: The attention weights are concentrated on prompt columns with the same TSR pattern and step index.

figures.description: Diagram showing how Transformers implement CoT by attending to similar examples at each reasoning step.

sections.title: Numerical experiments
content: Condition 1 is crucial for the success of ICL but not for CoT.
Training dynamics of CoT: same TSR & step matter.

figures.description: Line chart showing testing error decreasing as the number of context examples increases for CoT vs ICL.
Line chart showing attention weights converging to the correct TSR and step over training iterations.
